channel.title: WEIJIAN ZHANG on Weijian Zhang
channel.description: Recent content in WEIJIAN ZHANG on Weijian Zhang

item.title: Professional Resume
item.description:
Professional Summary
#

Results-oriented Software Engineer with a Master’s in Computer Engineering and extensive experience at Amazon Web Services (AWS). Proven track record in building cloud infrastructure from scratch, leading technical initiatives for high-scale container services (ECS/Fargate), and pioneering Agentic AI solutions to automate operational workflows.

Technical Skills
#

  • Languages: Java, Python, TypeScript
  • Cloud & Infrastructure: AWS (EC2, ECS, Fargate, Bedrock, Lambda), Docker
  • GenAI / ML: Agentic AI, AWS Bedrock, Strands Framework, Claude, Amazon Q
  • Tools: Git, AWS CDK

Experience
#

Amazon Web Services (AWS) May 2019 – Present
#

Bedrock / Agentic AI Jan 2024 – Present
#

  • Architected a multi-agent solution using AWS Bedrock and the Strands framework to automate the “General Limit Increase” lifecycle — reduced end-to-end resolution time by 75% and eliminated 20 hours of weekly engineering overhead
  • Pioneered a “solopreneur” workflow leveraging Claude and Amazon Q CLI to simulate a full development squad, accelerating the SDLC by 5x
  • Built and shipped a production-grade codebase (12k+ lines, 100+ commits) in just 30 days

Tech Lead, ECS Managed Instance Observability Service May 2019 – Dec 2023
#

  • Led a team of 7 to migrate from legacy WebSocket-based stateful systems to a stateless RESTful architecture, resolving a 100-connection-per-node bottleneck and mitigating 65k port exhaustion risks
  • Engineered an Async I/O model achieving a 10x increase in single-node concurrency to support 200,000+ instances and 10k RPS peak throughput
  • Developed a defense-in-depth authentication mechanism integrating IAM identity with VPC-source path validation
  • Reduced manual overhead and improved on-call incident response efficiency by 80% via CloudWatch Cross-Account Observability
  • Orchestrated end-to-end automation of Cell creation workflow, reducing delivery time from 6 months to 3 days (60x improvement)

Bosch 2015 – 2016
#

  • Designed and developed a custom Android application for the Boost Recuperation System with Bluetooth/CAN protocol connectivity
  • Developed a virtual testing environment for vehicle ECUs (ABS/ESP)

Education
#

Degree School Year
M.S. Computer Engineering New York University (NYU) 2017–2018
B.S. Computer Engineering Rose-Hulman Institute of Technology 2012–2015

item.title: The "Ghost" in the Machine — Solving IP Reuse
item.description:
In a large-scale cloud environment, resources are ephemeral. When an instance is terminated, its internal IP is released back into the pool and often reassigned to a new instance within minutes. This creates a classic Race Condition in our mapping service.

The Problem: The “Stale Delete”
#

Imagine this scenario:

  1. Instance A (IP: 10.0.0.1) is marked for deprovisioning. A “Delete” task is sent to the queue.
  2. The network jitters — the “Delete” task is delayed.
  3. Instance B is launched and gets the same IP: 10.0.0.1. It successfully registers itself in our DynamoDB mapping table.
  4. The delayed “Delete” task for Instance A finally arrives.

Without a safeguard, the service would simply see a delete request for 10.0.0.1 and wipe out the new registration for Instance B. Instance B is now a “ghost” — it exists in the cell but is invisible to the control plane.
